title: Max clients reached error with Postgres Trigger node
symptom: When using the Postgres Trigger node, n8n throws 'MaxClientsInSessionMode: max clients reached', causing executions to get stuck in the queue.
applies_when: Connected to Supabase with session-mode pooler
root_cause: The error occurs because the Postgres Trigger node requires a persistent connection, which conflicts with the session-mode connection pooling used by Supabase. This leads to reaching the maximum number of allowed clients, causing execution failures.
fix_steps: Switch to a direct connection string for Supabase
Kill any stale idle connections in Supabase’s SQL editor
agent_instruction: Change the connection method for the Postgres Trigger node to a direct connection or transaction-mode pooler.
